verses.latin_vulgate: Igitur post hæc fecit sibi Absalom currus, et equites, et quinquaginta viros qui præcederent eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיְהִי֙ מֵאַ֣חֲרֵי כֵ֔ן וַיַּ֤עַשׂ לוֹ֙ אַבְשָׁל֔וֹם מֶרְכָּבָ֖ה וְסֻסִ֑ים וַחֲמִשִּׁ֥ים אִ֖ישׁ רָצִ֥ים לְפָנָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now after these things Absalom made himself chariots, and horsemen, and fifty men to run before him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja tapahtui sitte, että Absalom antoi valmistaa itsellensä vaunut ja hevoset, ja viisikymmentä miestä, jotka olivat hänen edelläjuoksiansa.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass after this, that Absalom prepared him chariots and horses, and fifty men to run before him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et mane consurgens Absalom, stabat juxta introitum portæ, et omnem virum qui habebat negotium ut veniret ad regis judicium, vocabat Absalom ad se, et dicebat : De qua civitate es tu ? Qui respondens aiebat : Ex una tribu Israël ego sum servus tuus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהִשְׁכִּים֙ אַבְשָׁל֔וֹם וְעָמַ֕ד עַל־יַ֖ד דֶּ֣רֶךְ הַשָּׁ֑עַר וַיְהִ֡י כׇּל־הָאִ֣ישׁ אֲשֶֽׁר־יִֽהְיֶה־לּוֹ־רִיב֩ לָב֨וֹא אֶל־הַמֶּ֜לֶךְ לַמִּשְׁפָּ֗ט וַיִּקְרָ֨א אַבְשָׁל֤וֹם אֵלָיו֙ וַיֹּ֗אמֶר אֵֽי־מִזֶּ֥ה עִיר֙ אַ֔תָּה וַיֹּ֕אמֶר מֵאַחַ֥ד שִׁבְטֵֽי־יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל עַבְדֶּֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Absalom rising up early stood by the entrance of the gate, and when any man had business to come to the king's judgment, Absalom called him to him, and said: Of what city art thou? He answered, and said: Thy servant is of such tribe of Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Absalom nousi varhain huomeneltain ja seisoi porttitien vieressä, ja kuin jollakin oli asiaa tulla kuninkaan tykö oikeuden eteen, kutsui Absalom sen tykönsä ja sanoi: sinun palvelias on yhdestä Israelin sukukunnista,
verses.king_james: And Absalom rose up early, and stood beside the way of the gate: and it was so, that when any man that had a controversy came to the king for judgment, then Absalom called unto him, and said, Of what city art thou? And he said, Thy servant is of one of the tribes of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondebatque ei Absalom : Videntur mihi sermones tui boni et justi, sed non est qui te audiat constitutus a rege. Dicebatque Absalom :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר אֵלָיו֙ אַבְשָׁל֔וֹם רְאֵ֥ה דְבָרֶ֖יךָ טוֹבִ֣ים וּנְכֹחִ֑ים וְשֹׁמֵ֥עַ אֵין־לְךָ֖ מֵאֵ֥ת הַמֶּֽלֶךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Absalom answered him: Thy words seem to me good and just. But there is no man appointed by the king to hear thee. And Absalom said:
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Absalom sanoi hänelle: katso, sinun asias on oikia ja selkiä; mutta ei ole yksikään siellä kuninkaalta asetettu, joka sinua kuulis.
verses.king_james: And Absalom said unto him, See, thy matters are good and right; but there is no man deputed of the king to hear thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quis me constituat judicem super terram, ut ad me veniant omnes qui habent negotium, et juste judicem ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ אַבְשָׁל֔וֹם מִֽי־יְשִׂמֵ֥נִי שֹׁפֵ֖ט בָּאָ֑רֶץ וְעָלַ֗י יָב֛וֹא כׇּל־אִ֛ישׁ אֲשֶֽׁר־יִֽהְיֶה־לּוֹ־רִ֥יב וּמִשְׁפָּ֖ט וְהִצְדַּקְתִּֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: O that they would make me judge over the land, that all that have business might come to me, that I might do them justice.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Absalom sanoi: kuka minun asettais tuomariksi maalla, että jokainen tulis minun tyköni jolla jotain asiaa ja kaipaamista on, auttaakseni häntä oikeuteen?
verses.king_james: Absalom said moreover, Oh that I were made judge in the land, that every man which hath any suit or cause might come unto me, and I would do him justice!

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed et cum accederet ad eum homo ut salutaret illum, extendebat manum suam, et apprehendens osculabatur eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָיָה֙ בִּקְרׇב־אִ֔ישׁ לְהִֽשְׁתַּחֲוֺ֖ת ל֑וֹ וְשָׁלַ֧ח אֶת־יָד֛וֹ וְהֶחֱזִ֥יק ל֖וֹ וְנָ֥שַׁק לֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Moreover when any man came to him to salute him, he put forth his hand, and took him, and kissed him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin joku tuli hänen tykönsä kumartamaan häntä, ojensi hän kätensä ja tarttui häneen, ja antoi hänen suuta.
verses.king_james: And it was so, that when any man came nigh to him to do him obeisance, he put forth his hand, and took him, and kissed him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Faciebatque hoc omni Israël venienti ad judicium ut audiretur a rege, et sollicitabat corda virorum Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֨עַשׂ אַבְשָׁל֜וֹם כַּדָּבָ֤ר הַזֶּה֙ לְכׇל־יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל אֲשֶׁר־יָבֹ֥אוּ לַמִּשְׁפָּ֖ט אֶל־הַמֶּ֑לֶךְ וַיְגַנֵּב֙ אַבְשָׁל֔וֹם אֶת־לֵ֖ב אַנְשֵׁ֥י יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And this he did to all Israel that came for judgment, to be heard by the king, and he enticed the hearts of the men of Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin teki Absalom kaikelle Israelille, kuin he tulivat oikeuden eteen kuninkaan tykö; ja niin varasti Absalom Israelin miesten sydämet.
verses.king_james: And on this manner did Absalom to all Israel that came to the king for judgment: so Absalom stole the hearts of the men of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Post quadraginta autem annos, dixit Absalom ad regem David : Vadam, et reddam vota mea quæ vovi Domino in Hebron.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֕י מִקֵּ֖ץ אַרְבָּעִ֣ים שָׁנָ֑ה וַיֹּ֤אמֶר אַבְשָׁלוֹם֙ אֶל־הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ אֵ֣לְכָה נָּ֗א וַאֲשַׁלֵּ֛ם אֶת־נִדְרִ֛י אֲשֶׁר־נָדַ֥רְתִּי לַיהֹוָ֖ה בְּחֶבְרֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And after forty years, Absalom said to king David: Let me go, and pay my vows which I have vowed to the Lord in Hebron.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja tapahtui neljänkymmenen ajastajan perästä, että Absalom sanoi kuninkaalle: minä menisin ja täyttäisin lupaukseni, jonka minä lupasin Herralle Hebronissa.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass after forty years, that Absalom said unto the king, I pray thee, let me go and pay my vow, which I have vowed unto the Lord, in Hebron.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vovens enim vovit servus tuus cum esset in Gessur Syriæ, dicens : Si reduxerit me Dominus in Jerusalem, sacrificabo Domino.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי־נֵ֙דֶר֙ נָדַ֣ר עַבְדְּךָ֔ בְּשִׁבְתִּ֥י בִגְשׁ֛וּר בַּאֲרָ֖ם לֵאמֹ֑ר אִם־[יָשׁ֨וֹב] (ישיב) יְשִׁיבֵ֤נִי יְהֹוָה֙ יְר֣וּשָׁלַ֔͏ִם וְעָבַדְתִּ֖י אֶת־יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: For thy servant made a vow, when he was in Gessur of Syria, saying: If the Lord shall bring me again into Jerusalem, I will offer sacrifice to the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä silloin kuin minä asuin Gessurissa Syriassa, lupasi sinun palvelias lupauksen ja sanoi: kuin Herra tuo minun kerran Jerusalemiin jälleen, palvelen minä Herraa.
verses.king_james: For thy servant vowed a vow while I abode at Geshur in Syria, saying, If the Lord shall bring me again indeed to Jerusalem, then I will serve the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque ei rex David : Vade in pace. Et surrexit, et abiit in Hebron.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּֽאמֶר־ל֥וֹ הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ לֵ֣ךְ בְּשָׁל֑וֹם וַיָּ֖קׇם וַיֵּ֥לֶךְ חֶבְרֽוֹנָה׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And king David said to him: Go in peace. And he arose, and went to Hebron.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuningas sanoi hänelle: mene rauhassa! Ja hän nousi ja meni Hebroniin.
verses.king_james: And the king said unto him, Go in peace. So he arose, and went to Hebron.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misit autem Absalom exploratores in universas tribus Israël, dicens : Statim ut audieritis clangorem buccinæ, dicite : Regnavit Absalom in Hebron.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁלַ֤ח אַבְשָׁלוֹם֙ מְרַגְּלִ֔ים בְּכׇל־שִׁבְטֵ֥י יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל לֵאמֹ֑ר כְּשׇׁמְעֲכֶם֙ אֶת־ק֣וֹל הַשֹּׁפָ֔ר וַאֲמַרְתֶּ֕ם מָלַ֥ךְ אַבְשָׁל֖וֹם בְּחֶבְרֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Absalom sent spies into all the tribes of Israel, saying: As soon as you shall hear the sound of the trumpet, say ye: Absalom reigneth in Hebron.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Absalom oli lähettänyt vakoojat kaikkiin Israelin sukukuntiin, ja käski sanoa: kuin saatte kuulla torven äänen, niin sanokaat: Absalom on kuningas Hebronissa.
verses.king_james: But Absalom sent spies throughout all the tribes of Israel, saying, As soon as ye hear the sound of the trumpet, then ye shall say, Absalom reigneth in Hebron.

verses.latin_vulgate: Porro cum Absalom ierunt ducenti viri de Jerusalem vocati, euntes simplici corde, et causam penitus ignorantes.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֶת־אַבְשָׁל֗וֹם הָלְכ֞וּ מָאתַ֤יִם אִישׁ֙ מִיר֣וּשָׁלַ֔͏ִם קְרֻאִ֖ים וְהֹלְכִ֣ים לְתֻמָּ֑ם וְלֹ֥א יָדְע֖וּ כׇּל־דָּבָֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now there went with Absalom two hundred men out of Jerusalem that were called, going with simplicity of heart, and knowing nothing of the design.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta Absalomin kanssa meni kaksisataa miestä Jerusalemista, jotka olivat kutsutut; mutta ne menivät yksinkertaisuudessansa, eikä mitään asiasta tietäneet.
verses.king_james: And with Absalom went two hundred men out of Jerusalem, that were called; and they went in their simplicity, and they knew not any thing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Accersivit quoque Absalom Achitophel Gilonitem consiliarium David, de civitate sua Gilo. Cumque immolaret victimas, facta est conjuratio valida, populusque concurrens augebatur cum Absalom.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁלַ֣ח אַ֠בְשָׁל֠וֹם אֶת־אֲחִיתֹ֨פֶל הַגִּילֹנִ֜י יוֹעֵ֣ץ דָּוִ֗ד מֵֽעִירוֹ֙ מִגִּלֹ֔ה בְּזׇבְח֖וֹ אֶת־הַזְּבָחִ֑ים וַיְהִ֤י הַקֶּ֙שֶׁר֙ אַמִּ֔ץ וְהָעָ֛ם הוֹלֵ֥ךְ וָרָ֖ב אֶת־אַבְשָׁלֽוֹם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Absalom also sent for Achitophel the Gilonite, David's counsellor, from his city Gilo. And while he was offering sacrifices, there was a strong conspiracy, and the people running together increased with Absalom.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Absalom lähetti myös Ahitophelin Gilonilaisen, Davidin neuvonantajan, perään hänen kaupungistansa Gilosta, kuin hän uhria uhrasi; niin tehtiin juuri vahva liitto, ja paljo kansaa lisääntyi ja eneni Absalomin tykö.
verses.king_james: And Absalom sent for Ahithophel the Gilonite, David’s counsellor, from his city, even from Giloh, while he offered sacrifices. And the conspiracy was strong; for the people increased continually with Absalom.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit igitur nuntius ad David, dicens : Toto corde universus Israël sequitur Absalom.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹא֙ הַמַּגִּ֔יד אֶל־דָּוִ֖ד לֵאמֹ֑ר הָיָ֛ה לֶב־אִ֥ישׁ יִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל אַחֲרֵ֥י אַבְשָׁלֽוֹם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And there came a messenger to David, saying: All Israel with their whole heart followeth Absalom.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja yksi tuli ja ilmoitti sen Davidille, ja sanoi: Israelin miesten sydän on kääntynyt Absalomin jälkeen.
verses.king_james: And there came a messenger to David, saying, The hearts of the men of Israel are after Absalom.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait David servis suis qui erant cum eo in Jerusalem : Surgite, fugiamus : neque enim erit nobis effugium a facie Absalom : festinate egredi, ne forte veniens occupet nos, et impellat super nos ruinam, et percutiat civitatem in ore gladii.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר דָּ֠וִ֠ד לְכׇל־עֲבָדָ֨יו אֲשֶׁר־אִתּ֤וֹ בִירוּשָׁלַ֙͏ִם֙ ק֣וּמוּ וְנִבְרָ֔חָה כִּ֛י לֹא־תִֽהְיֶה־לָּ֥נוּ פְלֵיטָ֖ה מִפְּנֵ֣י אַבְשָׁלֹ֑ם מַהֲר֣וּ לָלֶ֗כֶת פֶּן־יְמַהֵ֤ר וְהִשִּׂגָ֙נוּ֙ וְהִדִּ֤יחַ עָלֵ֙ינוּ֙ אֶת־הָ֣רָעָ֔ה וְהִכָּ֥ה הָעִ֖יר לְפִי־חָֽרֶב׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David said to his servants, that were with him in Jerusalem: Arise and let us flee: for we shall not escape else from the face of Absalom: make haste to go out, lest he come and overtake us, and bring ruin upon us, and smite the city with the edge of the sword.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja David sanoi kaikille palvelioillensa, jotka hänen kanssansa Jerusalemissa olivat: nouskaamme ja paetkaamme, sillä muutoin ei pääse yksikään meistä Absalomin edestä! rientäkäät menemään, ettei hän äkisti karkaisi meidän päällemme, ja ottaisi meitä kiinni, ja vahingoitsisi meitä ja löisi kaupunkia miekalla.
verses.king_james: And David said unto all his servants that were with him at Jerusalem, Arise, and let us flee; for we shall not else escape from Absalom: make speed to depart, lest he overtake us suddenly, and bring evil upon us, and smite the city with the edge of the sword.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixeruntque servi regis ad eum : Omnia quæcumque præceperit dominus noster rex, libenter exequemur servi tui.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּאמְר֥וּ עַבְדֵֽי־הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ אֶל־הַמֶּ֑לֶךְ כְּכֹ֧ל אֲשֶׁר־יִבְחַ֛ר אֲדֹנִ֥י הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ הִנֵּ֥ה עֲבָדֶֽיךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the king's servants said to him: Whatsoever our lord the king shall command, we thy servants will willingly execute.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuninkaan palveliat sanoivat kuninkaalle: mitä meidän herramme kuningas valitsee, katso tässä me palvelias olemme.
verses.king_james: And the king’s servants said unto the king, Behold, thy servants are ready to do whatsoever my lord the king shall appoint.

verses.latin_vulgate: Egressus est ergo rex et universa domus ejus pedibus suis : et dereliquit rex decem mulieres concubinas ad custodiendam domum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּצֵ֥א הַמֶּ֛לֶךְ וְכׇל־בֵּית֖וֹ בְּרַגְלָ֑יו וַיַּעֲזֹ֣ב הַמֶּ֗לֶךְ אֵ֣ת עֶ֧שֶׂר נָשִׁ֛ים פִּֽלַגְשִׁ֖ים לִשְׁמֹ֥ר הַבָּֽיִת׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the king went forth, and all his household on foot: and the king left ten women his concubines to keep the house:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuningas kävi jalkaisin ulos kaiken huoneensa kanssa; mutta hän jätti kymmenen jalkavaimoa huoneita vartioitsemaan.
verses.king_james: And the king went forth, and all his household after him. And the king left ten women, which were concubines, to keep the house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Egressusque rex et omnis Israël pedibus suis, stetit procul a domo :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּצֵ֥א הַמֶּ֛לֶךְ וְכׇל־הָעָ֖ם בְּרַגְלָ֑יו וַיַּעַמְד֖וּ בֵּ֥ית הַמֶּרְחָֽק׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the king going forth and all Israel on foot, stood afar off from the house:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin kuningas ja kaikki kansa olivat jalkaisin käyneet ulos, seisoivat he kaukana huoneista.
verses.king_james: And the king went forth, and all the people after him, and tarried in a place that was far off.

verses.latin_vulgate: et universi servi ejus ambulabant juxta eum, et legiones Cerethi, et Phelethi, et omnes Gethæi, pugnatores validi, sexcenti viri qui secuti eum fuerant de Geth pedites, præcedebant regem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְכׇל־עֲבָדָיו֙ עֹבְרִ֣ים עַל־יָד֔וֹ וְכׇל־הַכְּרֵתִ֖י וְכׇל־הַפְּלֵתִ֑י וְכׇֽל־הַגִּתִּ֞ים שֵׁשׁ־מֵא֣וֹת אִ֗ישׁ אֲשֶׁר־בָּ֤אוּ בְרַגְלוֹ֙ מִגַּ֔ת עֹבְרִ֖ים עַל־פְּנֵ֥י הַמֶּֽלֶךְ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And all his servants walked by him, and the bands of the Cerethi, and the Phelethi, and all the Gethites, valiant warriors, six hundred men who had followed him from Geth on foot, went before the king.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kaikki hänen palveliansa kävivät hänen tykönänsä, sitälikin myös kaikki Kretit ja Pletit; ja kaikki Gatilaiset kuusisataa miestä, jotka jalkaisin olivat tulleet Gatista, kävivät kuninkaan edellä.
verses.king_james: And all his servants passed on beside him; and all the Cherethites, and all the Pelethites, and all the Gittites, six hundred men which came after him from Gath, passed on before the king.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit autem rex ad Ethai Gethæum : Cur venis nobiscum ? revertere, et habita cum rege, quia peregrinus es, et egressus es de loco tuo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר הַמֶּ֙לֶךְ֙ אֶל־אִתַּ֣י הַגִּתִּ֔י לָ֧מָּה תֵלֵ֛ךְ גַּם־אַתָּ֖ה אִתָּ֑נוּ שׁ֣וּב וְשֵׁ֤ב עִם־הַמֶּ֙לֶךְ֙ כִּי־נׇכְרִ֣י אַ֔תָּה וְגַם־גֹּלֶ֥ה אַתָּ֖ה לִמְקוֹמֶֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the king said to Ethai the Gethite: Why comest thou with us: return and dwell with the king, for thou art a stranger, and art come out of thy own place.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuningas sanoi Ittaille Gatilaiselle: miksi sinä myös tulet meidän kanssamme? Palaja ja ole kuninkaan tykönä; sillä sinä olet muukalainen, ja olet tänne tullut sinun sialtas.
verses.king_james: Then said the king to Ittai the Gittite, Wherefore goest thou also with us? return to thy place, and abide with the king: for thou art a stranger, and also an exile.

verses.latin_vulgate: Heri venisti, et hodie compelleris nobiscum egredi ? ego autem vadam quo iturus sum : revertere, et reduc tecum fratres tuos, et Dominus faciet tecum misericordiam et veritatem, quia ostendisti gratiam et fidem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תְּמ֣וֹל ׀ בּוֹאֶ֗ךָ וְהַיּ֞וֹם (אנועך) [אֲנִֽיעֲךָ֤] עִמָּ֙נוּ֙ לָלֶ֔כֶת וַאֲנִ֣י הוֹלֵ֔ךְ עַ֥ל אֲשֶׁר־אֲנִ֖י הוֹלֵ֑ךְ שׁ֣וּב וְהָשֵׁ֧ב אֶת־אַחֶ֛יךָ עִמָּ֖ךְ חֶ֥סֶד וֶאֱמֶֽת׃
verses.douay_rheims: Yesterday thou camest, and to day shalt thou be forced to go forth with us? but I shall go whither I am going: return thou, and take back thy brethren with thee, and the Lord will shew thee mercy, and truth, because thou hast shewn grace and fidelity.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä eilen sinä tulit, ja minun pitäis tänäpänä antaman sinun käydä meidän kanssamme; mutta minä menen, kuhunka minä menen: palaja sinä ja vie sinun veljes myötäs. Sinun kanssas tapahtukoon armo ja vakuus!
verses.king_james: Whereas thou camest but yesterday, should I this day make thee go up and down with us? seeing I go whither I may, return thou, and take back thy brethren: mercy and truth be with thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respondit Ethai regi dicens : Vivit Dominus, et vivit dominus meus rex, quoniam in quocumque loco fueris, domine mi rex, sive in morte, sive in vita, ibi erit servus tuus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֧עַן אִתַּ֛י אֶת־הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ וַיֹּאמַ֑ר חַי־יְהֹוָ֗ה וְחֵי֙ אֲדֹנִ֣י הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ כִּ֠י (אם) בִּמְק֞וֹם אֲשֶׁ֥ר יִהְיֶה־שָּׁ֣ם ׀ אֲדֹנִ֣י הַמֶּ֗לֶךְ אִם־לְמָ֙וֶת֙ אִם־לְחַיִּ֔ים כִּי־שָׁ֖ם יִהְיֶ֥ה עַבְדֶּֽךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Ethai answered the king, saying: As the Lord liveth, and as my lord the king liveth: in what place soever thou shalt be, my lord, O king, either in death, or in life, there will thy servant be.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ittai vastasi kuningasta ja sanoi: niin totta kuin Herra elää, ja niin totta kuin minun herrani kuningas elää, kussa ikänä herrani kuningas on, olisi se kuolemassa eli elämässä, siellä pitää myös sinun palvelias oleman.
verses.king_james: And Ittai answered the king, and said, As the Lord liveth, and as my lord the king liveth, surely in what place my lord the king shall be, whether in death or life, even there also will thy servant be.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait David Ethai : Veni, et transi. Et transivit Ethai Gethæus, et omnes viri qui cum eo erant, et reliqua multitudo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֧אמֶר דָּוִ֛ד אֶל־אִתַּ֖י לֵ֣ךְ וַעֲבֹ֑ר וַֽיַּעֲבֹ֞ר אִתַּ֤י הַגִּתִּי֙ וְכׇל־אֲנָשָׁ֔יו וְכׇל־הַטַּ֖ף אֲשֶׁ֥ר אִתּֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David said to Ethai: Come, and pass over. And Ethai the Gethite passed, and all the men that were with him, and the rest of the people.
verses.finnish_biblia: David sanoi Ittaille: tule ja käy meidän kanssamme. Ja niin kävi Ittai Gatilainen, ja kaikki hänen miehensä, ja kaikki lapset jotka hänen kanssansa olivat.
verses.king_james: And David said to Ittai, Go and pass over. And Ittai the Gittite passed over, and all his men, and all the little ones that were with him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnesque flebant voce magna, et universus populus transibat : rex quoque transgrediebatur torrentem Cedron, et cunctus populus incedebat contra viam quæ respicit ad desertum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְכׇל־הָאָ֗רֶץ בּוֹכִים֙ ק֣וֹל גָּד֔וֹל וְכׇל־הָעָ֖ם עֹבְרִ֑ים וְהַמֶּ֗לֶךְ עֹבֵר֙ בְּנַ֣חַל קִדְר֔וֹן וְכׇל־הָעָם֙ עֹבְרִ֔ים עַל־פְּנֵי־דֶ֖רֶךְ אֶת־הַמִּדְבָּֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they all wept with a loud voice, and all the people passed over: the king also himself went over the brook Cedron, and all the people marched towards the way that looketh to the desert.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kaikki maakunta itki suurella äänellä, ja kaikki väki meni kanssa. Ja kuningas kävi Kidronin ojan ylitse, ja kaikki kansa kävi edellä korven tietä.
verses.king_james: And all the country wept with a loud voice, and all the people passed over: the king also himself passed over the brook Kidron, and all the people passed over, toward the way of the wilderness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit autem et Sadoc sacerdos, et universi Levitæ cum eo, portantes arcam fœderis Dei : et deposuerunt arcam Dei. Et ascendit Abiathar, donec expletus esset omnis populus qui egressus fuerat de civitate.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהִנֵּ֨ה גַם־צָד֜וֹק וְכׇֽל־הַלְוִיִּ֣ם אִתּ֗וֹ נֹֽשְׂאִים֙ אֶת־אֲרוֹן֙ בְּרִ֣ית הָאֱלֹהִ֔ים וַיַּצִּ֙קוּ֙ אֶת־אֲר֣וֹן הָאֱלֹהִ֔ים וַיַּ֖עַל אֶבְיָתָ֑ר עַד־תֹּ֥ם כׇּל־הָעָ֖ם לַעֲב֥וֹר מִן־הָעִֽיר׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And Sadoc the priest also came, and all the Levites with him carrying the ark of the covenant of God, and they set down the ark of God: and Abiathar went up, till all the people that was come out of the city had done passing.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja katso, Zadok myös oli siellä ja kaikki Leviläiset, jotka hänen kanssansa olivat, ja he kantoivat Jumalan liitonarkin, ja panivat ylös Jumalan arkin. Ja Abjatar meni ylös, siihenasti kuin kaikki väki tuli kaupungista.
verses.king_james: And lo Zadok also, and all the Levites were with him, bearing the ark of the covenant of God: and they set down the ark of God; and Abiathar went up, until all the people had done passing out of the city.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit rex ad Sadoc : Reporta arcam Dei in urbem : si invenero gratiam in oculis Domini, reducet me, et ostendet mihi eam, et tabernaculum suum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר הַמֶּ֙לֶךְ֙ לְצָד֔וֹק הָשֵׁ֛ב אֶת־אֲר֥וֹן הָאֱלֹהִ֖ים הָעִ֑יר אִם־אֶמְצָ֥א חֵן֙ בְּעֵינֵ֣י יְהֹוָ֔ה וֶהֱשִׁבַ֕נִי וְהִרְאַ֥נִי אֹת֖וֹ וְאֶת־נָוֵֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the king said to Sadoc: Carry back the ark of God into the city: if I shall find grace in the sight of the Lord, he will bring me again, and he will shew me it, and his tabernacle.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoi kuningas Zadokille: kanna Jumalan arkki kaupunkiin jälleen: jos minä löydän armon Herran edessä, niin hän tuo minun jälleen ja antaa minun nähdä hänen ja hänen majansa.
verses.king_james: And the king said unto Zadok, Carry back the ark of God into the city: if I shall find favour in the eyes of the Lord, he will bring me again, and shew me both it, and his habitation:

verses.latin_vulgate: Si autem dixerit mihi : Non places : præsto sum : faciat quod bonum est coram se.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאִם֙ כֹּ֣ה יֹאמַ֔ר לֹ֥א חָפַ֖צְתִּי בָּ֑ךְ הִנְנִ֕י יַעֲשֶׂה־לִּ֕י כַּאֲשֶׁ֖ר ט֥וֹב בְּעֵינָֽיו׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: But if he shall say to me: Thou pleasest me not: I am ready, let him do that which is good before him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta jos hän niin sanoo: et sinä ole minulle otollinen! katso, tässä minä olen: hän tehköön minulle, mitä hän tahtoo!
verses.king_james: But if he thus say, I have no delight in thee; behold, here am I, let him do to me as seemeth good unto him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit rex ad Sadoc sacerdotem : O videns, revertere in civitatem in pace : et Achimaas filius tuus, et Jonathas filius Abiathar, duo filii vestri, sint vobiscum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר הַמֶּ֙לֶךְ֙ אֶל־צָד֣וֹק הַכֹּהֵ֔ן הֲרוֹאֶ֣ה אַתָּ֔ה שֻׁ֥בָה הָעִ֖יר בְּשָׁל֑וֹם וַאֲחִימַ֨עַץ בִּנְךָ֜ וִיהוֹנָתָ֧ן בֶּן־אֶבְיָתָ֛ר שְׁנֵ֥י בְנֵיכֶ֖ם אִתְּכֶֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the king said to Sadoc the priest: O seer, return into the city in peace: and let Achimaas thy son, and Jonathan the son of Abiathar, your two sons, be with you.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuningas sanoi papille Zadokille: sinä näkiä, palaja rauhassa kaupunkiin, ja molemmat teidän poikanne teidän kanssanne, Ahimaats sinun poikas ja Jonatan AbJatarin poika.
verses.king_james: The king said also unto Zadok the priest, Art not thou a seer? return into the city in peace, and your two sons with you, Ahimaaz thy son, and Jonathan the son of Abiathar.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce ego abscondar in campestribus deserti, donec veniat sermo a vobis indicans mihi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רְאוּ֙ אָנֹכִ֣י מִתְמַהְמֵ֔הַּ (בעברות) [בְּעַֽרְב֖וֹת] הַמִּדְבָּ֑ר עַ֣ד בּ֥וֹא דָבָ֛ר מֵעִמָּכֶ֖ם לְהַגִּ֥יד לִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Behold I will lie hid in the plains of the wilderness, till there come word from you to certify me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Katso, minä odotan tasaisella kedolla korvessa siihenasti kuin teiltä sana tulee, tekemään minulle tiettäväksi.
verses.king_james: See, I will tarry in the plain of the wilderness, until there come word from you to certify me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Reportaverunt ergo Sadoc et Abiathar arcam Dei in Jerusalem, et manserunt ibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֨שֶׁב צָד֧וֹק וְאֶבְיָתָ֛ר אֶת־אֲר֥וֹן הָאֱלֹהִ֖ים יְרוּשָׁלָ֑͏ִם וַיֵּשְׁב֖וּ שָֽׁם׃
verses.douay_rheims: So Sadoc and Abiathar carried back the ark of God into Jerusalem: and they tarried there.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Zadok ja AbJatar kantoivat Jumalan arkin Jerusalemiin, ja olivat siellä.
verses.king_james: Zadok therefore and Abiathar carried the ark of God again to Jerusalem: and they tarried there.

verses.latin_vulgate: Porro David ascendebat clivum Olivarum, scandens et flens, nudis pedibus incedens, et operto capite : sed et omnis populus qui erat cum eo, operto capite ascendebat plorans.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְדָוִ֡ד עֹלֶה֩ בְמַעֲלֵ֨ה הַזֵּיתִ֜ים עֹלֶ֣ה ׀ וּבוֹכֶ֗ה וְרֹ֥אשׁ לוֹ֙ חָפ֔וּי וְה֖וּא הֹלֵ֣ךְ יָחֵ֑ף וְכׇל־הָעָ֣ם אֲשֶׁר־אִתּ֗וֹ חָפוּ֙ אִ֣ישׁ רֹאשׁ֔וֹ וְעָל֥וּ עָלֹ֖ה וּבָכֹֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: But David went up by the ascent of mount Olivet, going up and weeping, walking barefoot, and with his head covered, and all the people that were with them, went up with their heads covered weeping.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta David meni ylös öljymäen paltaa myöten ja itki käydessänsä, ja hänen päänsä oli peitetty, ja kävi kengittä; niin myös kaikki kansa, jotka hänen kanssansa olivat, peittivät päänsä, ja käyden astuivat ylös ja itkivät.
verses.king_james: And David went up by the ascent of mount Olivet, and wept as he went up, and had his head covered, and he went barefoot: and all the people that was with him covered every man his head, and they went up, weeping as they went up.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nuntiatum est autem David quod et Achitophel esset in conjuratione cum Absalom : dixitque David : Infatua, quæso, Domine, consilium Achitophel.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְדָוִד֙ הִגִּ֣יד לֵאמֹ֔ר אֲחִיתֹ֥פֶל בַּקֹּשְׁרִ֖ים עִם־אַבְשָׁל֑וֹם וַיֹּ֣אמֶר דָּוִ֔ד סַכֶּל־נָ֛א אֶת־עֲצַ֥ת אֲחִיתֹ֖פֶל יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And it was told David that Achitophel also was in the conspiracy with Absalom, and David said: Infatuate, O Lord, I beseech thee, the counsel of Achitophel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin Davidille ilmoitettiin Ahitophelin olevan liitossa Absalomin kanssa, sanoi hän: Herra, tee Ahitophelin neuvo tyhmyydeksi!
verses.king_james: And one told David, saying, Ahithophel is among the conspirators with Absalom. And David said, O Lord, I pray thee, turn the counsel of Ahithophel into foolishness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque ascenderet David summitatem montis in quo adoraturus erat Dominum, ecce occurrit ei Chusai Arachites, scissa veste, et terra pleno capite.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֤י דָוִד֙ בָּ֣א עַד־הָרֹ֔אשׁ אֲשֶֽׁר־יִשְׁתַּחֲוֶ֥ה שָׁ֖ם לֵאלֹהִ֑ים וְהִנֵּ֤ה לִקְרָאתוֹ֙ חוּשַׁ֣י הָאַרְכִּ֔י קָר֙וּעַ֙ כֻּתׇּנְתּ֔וֹ וַאֲדָמָ֖ה עַל־רֹאשֽׁוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when David was come to the top of the mountain, where he was about to adore the Lord, behold Chusai the Arachite, came to meet him with his garment rent and his head covered with earth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin David tuli kukkulalle, kussa Jumalaa rukoiltiin, katso, Husai Arkilainen kohtasi häntä reväistyllä hameella, ja multaa hänen päänsä päällä.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, that when David was come to the top of the mount, where he worshipped God, behold, Hushai the Archite came to meet him with his coat rent, and earth upon his head:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit ei David : Si veneris mecum, eris mihi oneri :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר ל֖וֹ דָּוִ֑ד אִ֚ם עָבַ֣רְתָּ אִתִּ֔י וְהָיִ֥תָ עָלַ֖י לְמַשָּֽׂא׃
verses.douay_rheims: And David said to him: If thou come with me, thou wilt be a burden to me:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja David sanoi: jos seuraat minua, niin sinä olet minulle kuormaksi.
verses.king_james: Unto whom David said, If thou passest on with me, then thou shalt be a burden unto me:

verses.latin_vulgate: si autem in civitatem revertaris, et dixeris Absalom : Servus tuus sum, rex : sicut fui servus patris tui, sic ero servus tuus : dissipabis consilium Achitophel.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאִם־הָעִ֣יר תָּשׁ֗וּב וְאָמַרְתָּ֤ לְאַבְשָׁלוֹם֙ עַבְדְּךָ֨ אֲנִ֤י הַמֶּ֙לֶךְ֙ אֶֽהְיֶ֔ה עֶ֣בֶד אָבִ֤יךָ וַֽאֲנִי֙ מֵאָ֔ז וְעַתָּ֖ה וַאֲנִ֣י עַבְדֶּ֑ךָ וְהֵפַרְתָּ֣ה לִ֔י אֵ֖ת עֲצַ֥ת אֲחִיתֹֽפֶל׃
verses.douay_rheims: But if thou return into the city, and wilt say to Absalom: I am thy servant, O king: as I have been thy father's servant, so I will be thy servant: thou shalt defeat the counsel of Achitophel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta jos palajat kaupunkiin ja sanot AbsalomilIe: minä olen sinun palvelias, kuningas, joka ennen isääs olen kauvan palvellut, ja tahdon olla nyt sinun palvelias: ja näin sinä minulle teet Ahitophelin neuvon tyhjäksi.
verses.king_james: But if thou return to the city, and say unto Absalom, I will be thy servant, O king; as I have been thy father’s servant hitherto, so will I now also be thy servant: then mayest thou for me defeat the counsel of Ahithophel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Habes autem tecum Sadoc et Abiathar sacerdotes : et omne verbum quodcumque audieris de domo regis, indicabis Sadoc et Abiathar sacerdotibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַהֲל֤וֹא עִמְּךָ֙ שָׁ֔ם צָד֥וֹק וְאֶבְיָתָ֖ר הַכֹּהֲנִ֑ים וְהָיָ֗ה כׇּל־הַדָּבָר֙ אֲשֶׁ֤ר תִּשְׁמַע֙ מִבֵּ֣ית הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ תַּגִּ֕יד לְצָד֥וֹק וּלְאֶבְיָתָ֖ר הַכֹּהֲנִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And thou hast with thee Sadoc, and soever thou shalt hear out of the king's house, thou shalt tell it to Sadoc and Abiathar the priests.
verses.finnish_biblia: Eikö Zadok ja AbJatar papit ole sinun kanssas? Ja kaikki, mitäs kuulet kuninkaan huoneessa, ilmoita papeille Zadokille ja AbJatarille.
verses.king_james: And hast thou not there with thee Zadok and Abiathar the priests? therefore it shall be, that what thing soever thou shalt hear out of the king’s house, thou shalt tell it to Zadok and Abiathar the priests.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sunt autem cum eis duo filii eorum Achimaas filius Sadoc, et Jonathas filius Abiathar : et mittetis per eos ad me omne verbum quod audieritis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנֵּה־שָׁ֤ם עִמָּם֙ שְׁנֵ֣י בְנֵיהֶ֔ם אֲחִימַ֣עַץ לְצָד֔וֹק וִיהוֹנָתָ֖ן לְאֶבְיָתָ֑ר וּשְׁלַחְתֶּ֤ם בְּיָדָם֙ אֵלַ֔י כׇּל־דָּבָ֖ר אֲשֶׁ֥ר תִּשְׁמָֽעוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And there are with them their two sons Achimaas; the son of Sadoc, and Jonathan the son of Abiathar: and you shall send by them to me every thing that you shall hear.
verses.finnish_biblia: Katso, heidän kaksi poikaansa ovat siellä heidän kanssansa, Ahimaats Zadokin poika ja Jonatan AbJatarin poika: niiden kanssa te saatte lähettää minulle, mitä te kuulette.
verses.king_james: Behold, they have there with them their two sons, Ahimaaz Zadok’s son, and Jonathan Abiathar’s son; and by them ye shall send unto me every thing that ye can hear.

verses.latin_vulgate: Veniente ergo Chusai amico David in civitatem, Absalom quoque ingressus est Jerusalem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֥א חוּשַׁ֛י רֵעֶ֥ה דָוִ֖ד הָעִ֑יר וְאַ֨בְשָׁל֔וֹם יָב֖וֹא יְרֽוּשָׁלָֽ͏ִם׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Then Chusai the friend of David went into the city, and Absalom came into Jerusalem.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Husai Davidin ystävä meni kaupunkiin ja Absalom tuli Jerusalemiin.
verses.king_james: So Hushai David’s friend came into the city, and Absalom came into Jerusalem.
